skill.name: performance-profiler
skill.description: Reviews code for common performance bottlenecks, including N+1 queries, inefficient loops, memory leaks, slow algorithms, and frontend rendering issues.
skill.summary: Finds common code performance issues and suggests practical fixes.
skill.author: CrazyDubya

security_audit.summary: The static findings were reviewed in context and appear to be false positives from Markdown examples, code fences, SQL samples, and performance terminology. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, executable scripts, network access, or unauthorized tool use was found. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk.

low_findings.title: Static finding dismissed: weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: The flagged line is the skill description about slow algorithms. It does not reference cryptography, hashing, encryption, or weak algorithms such as MD5 or SHA1.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains performance-focused metadata only. I found no cryptographic operation or recommendation in the surrounding content.

low_findings.title: Static finding dismissed: Markdown backticks treated as command execution
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are fenced code examples and inline documentation for performance review. They are not scripts executed by the skill, and the skill only allows Read, Grep, and Glob.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every location is Markdown documentation or an example snippet. There is no shell execution mechanism or tool permission that would run these snippets.

low_findings.title: Static finding dismissed: dynamic import example
low_findings.description: The dynamic import appears in frontend optimization advice for lazy loading. It is an example for users to inspect or suggest, not code that the skill executes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a suggested React lazy loading pattern. No package loading, remote import, or runtime execution path is present in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Static finding dismissed: reconnaissance patterns in examples
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are SQL examples, algorithm examples, checklist text, and index guidance. They do not collect system information or enumerate host resources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are standard performance-analysis examples and checklist items. I found no commands for host discovery, credential discovery, or environment enumeration.

content.value_statement: Slow applications are hard to improve without a structured review. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code inspect code for common bottlenecks and practical fixes.
actual_capabilities: Reviews code for N+1 query patterns and missing eager loading.
Identifies inefficient loops, repeated calculations, and poor data structures.
Checks frontend code for avoidable re-renders and missing lazy loading.
Flags common memory leak patterns such as uncleared timers and listeners.
Produces prioritized performance reports with impact and fix guidance.
Suggests profiling tools for JavaScript, Python, and databases.
limitations: It does not run benchmarks or execute profiling tools.
It depends on the code and context provided by the user.
It may miss bottlenecks caused by production traffic or infrastructure.
It gives guidance, not guaranteed performance improvements.

use_cases.title: Review a slow endpoint
use_cases.description: Find database and algorithm bottlenecks before changing backend code.

use_cases.title: Improve page responsiveness
use_cases.description: Check React components for re-render, bundle, image, and list rendering issues.

use_cases.title: Prepare a performance review
use_cases.description: Create a prioritized report that explains impact, location, and recommended fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Basic code review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when a single file or function feels slow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code for performance bottlenecks. Focus on the most likely causes of slowness and suggest practical fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Database bottleneck review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this for API handlers, services, or repository code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this backend code for database performance issues, especially N+1 queries, missing indexes, large payloads, and missing pagination.

prompt_templates.title: Frontend rendering review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this for React screens, component trees, or slow lists.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this frontend code for rendering performance problems. Check memoization, object creation, image loading, bundle size, and virtual scrolling needs.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritized optimization plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this before planning a performance sprint.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prioritized performance report for this codebase. Include likely impact, evidence, fix strategy, and how to measure each improvement.

output_examples.input: A user list endpoint loads each user's posts inside a loop.
output_examples.output: Critical issue: likely N+1 queries. Replace per-user post loading with eager loading or a join, then verify query count before and after.

output_examples.input: A React table renders thousands of rows and creates inline objects in each render.
output_examples.output: High priority: render work scales with row count. Add virtualization, stabilize props, and measure with the React profiler.

output_examples.input: A data processor compares two arrays with nested loops.
output_examples.output: High priority: nested loops create quadratic growth. Build a lookup map for one array and reduce matching to linear time.

best_practices: Share representative code paths and recent performance symptoms.
Measure baseline performance before applying suggested changes.
Prioritize fixes by user impact, frequency, and implementation risk.
anti_patterns: Do not optimize code without a measured or clearly observed problem.
Do not apply every suggestion without checking correctness and readability.
Do not use it as a replacement for production profiling data.

faq.question: Does this skill run performance tests?
faq.answer: No. It reviews code and suggests profiling methods, but it does not execute benchmarks.

faq.question: Can it find N+1 query problems?
faq.answer: Yes. It specifically looks for queries inside loops and suggests eager loading or joins.

faq.question: Does it support frontend performance work?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers re-renders, lazy loading, images, bundle size, and long list rendering.

faq.question: Can it prove a fix will improve speed?
faq.answer: No. It can estimate likely impact, but users should measure before and after.

faq.question: What tools does the skill use?
faq.answer: The skill declares Read, Grep, and Glob for code inspection.

faq.question: Is it suitable for code review?
faq.answer: Yes. It can produce a prioritized report with issues, impact, and recommended fixes.
